page: Microsoft Azure EC128ies_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the ECIEV5 family. It is powered by an Intel processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 128 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 6.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include local storage, requiring the use of external storage options, though it is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are zero integrated GPUs, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel computing workloads. Additionally, the instance does not provide any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC128ies_v5 offers high resource density and isolation, which is beneficial for enterprise workloads requiring dedicated compute resources. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, focusing the value proposition entirely on memory capacity and dedicated CPU allocation. This configuration is engineered for large-scale databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and workloads requiring isolated virtualization environments.
description: Microsoft Azure EC128ies_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance from the ECIEV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable, and contains zero GPUs. The lack of local storage and hardware acceleration represents a design tradeoff that prioritizes high-density dedicated compute and memory resources. Consequently, the EC128ies_v5 is structured for large-scale databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and workloads requiring isolated environments.
